one_time_description: Robinson Huron Treaty settlement receivable - past compensation from Federal and Ontario Provincial Governments for breach of Robinson Huron Treaty of 1850 obligations
economic_notes: Mississaugi Retail Cannabis Corporation is a 100%-owned government business enterprise accounted for on modified equity basis. Revenue $1,530,408 and net income $122,798 for 2024. Band also has housing loan program and on-reserve home renovation loan program.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to management not completing assessment of tangible capital assets for potential asset retirement obligations under PS 3280 (effective April 1, 2022); impact on expenses and annual surplus cannot be quantified. Major one-time item: Robinson Huron Treaty settlement of $369,066,634 recorded as receivable in 2024, dominating annual surplus and accumulated surplus. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $10,570,807 + Gov Canada Other $624,647 + Province of Ontario $2,214,256 + North Shore Tribal Council $1,353,793 + OFNLP $1,126,064 + Robinson Huron Treaty $369,066,634 + Mississagi Trust $107,709 + Amounts from Trust Funds $18,364 = $384,082,274; OSR includes band enterprises $547,344 + other revenue $3,431,607 + income from GBE $129,286 + gain on investments $205 + trust amounts = approximately $4,560,769. Mississaugi Retail Cannabis Corporation is accounted for on modified equity basis (1 GBE). No schedule of remuneration disclosed in the extracted text. Social Services expenses $1,684,865 and Capital expenses $102,857 also reported in segment disclosure.
